info.title: ConfigCat CLI
info.description: The ConfigCat Command Line Interface allows you to interact with the ConfigCat Management API via the command line. It supports most functionality found on the ConfigCat Dashboard. You can manage ConfigCat resources like Feature Flags, Targeting / Percentage rules, Products, Configs, Environments, and more.

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription:
This is the Command Line Tool of ConfigCat.
ConfigCat is a hosted feature flag service: https://configcat.com
For more information, see the documentation here: https://configcat.com/docs/advanced/cli

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: setup
commands.description: Setup the CLI with Public Management API host and credentials

options.name: api-host
options.description: The Management API host, also used from CONFIGCAT_API_HOST. (default 'api.configcat.com')

options.name: username
options.description: The Management API basic authentication username, also used from CONFIGCAT_API_USER

options.name: password
options.description: The Management API basic authentication password, also used from CONFIGCAT_API_PASS

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a new Feature Flag or Setting in a specified Config identified by the `--config-id` option

options.name: config-id
options.description: ID of the Config where the Flag must be created

options.name: name
options.description: Name of the new Flag or Setting

options.name: key
options.description: Key of the new Flag or Setting (must be unique within the given Config)

options.name: hint
options.description: Hint of the new Flag or Setting

options.name: init-values-per-environment
options.description: Initial value for specific Environments. Format: `<environment-id>:<initial-value>`

options.name: predefined-variations
options.description: Predefined variations of the Feature Flag or Setting. Format: `<value>` or `<name>:<value>`.

options.name: type
options.description: Type of the new Flag or Setting

options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: targeting
commands.description: Manage V2 Feature Flag & Setting targeting options

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: rule
commands.description: Manage targeting rules

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create new targeting rule

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: user
commands.description: Create user based targeting rule

options.name: --flag-id
options.description: ID of the Feature Flag or Setting

options.name: environment-id
options.description: ID of the Environment where the rule must be created

options.name: attribute
options.description: The User Object attribute that the condition is based on

options.name: comparator
options.description: The operator which defines the relation between the comparison attribute and the comparison value

options.name: comparison-value
options.description: The value that the User Object attribute is compared to. Can be a double, string, or value-hint list in the format: `<value>:<hint>`

options.name: served-value
options.description: The value associated with the targeting rule. Leave it empty if the targeting rule has percentage options. It must respect the setting type. When the flag has Predefined Variations, it must be either the Variation's ID or name

options.name: reason
options.description: The reason note for the Audit Log if the Product's 'Config changes require a reason' preference is turned on

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: segment
commands.description: Create segment based targeting rule

options.name: --flag-id
options.description: ID of the Feature Flag or Setting

options.name: environment-id
options.description: ID of the Environment where the rule must be created

options.name: comparator
options.description: The operator which defines the expected result of the evaluation of the segment

options.name: segment-id
options.description: ID of the segment that the condition is based on

options.name: served-value
options.description: The value associated with the targeting rule. Leave it empty if the targeting rule has percentage options. It must respect the setting type. When the flag has Predefined Variations, it must be either the Variation's ID or name

options.name: reason
options.description: The reason note for the Audit Log if the Product's 'Config changes require a reason' preference is turned on

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: prerequisite
commands.description: Create prerequisite flag based targeting rule

options.name: --flag-id
options.description: ID of the Feature Flag or Setting

options.name: environment-id
options.description: ID of the Environment where the rule must be created

options.name: comparator
options.description: The operator which defines the relation between the evaluated value of the prerequisite flag and the comparison value

options.name: prerequisite-id
options.description: ID of the prerequisite flag that the condition is based on

options.name: prerequisite-value
options.description: The evaluated value of the prerequisite flag is compared to. It must respect the prerequisite flag's setting type. When the prerequisite flag has Predefined Variations, it either must be the Variation's ID or name

options.name: served-value
options.description: The value associated with the targeting rule. Leave it empty if the targeting rule has percentage options. It must respect the setting type. When the flag has Predefined Variations, it must be either the Variation's ID or name

options.name: reason
options.description: The reason note for the Audit Log if the Product's 'Config changes require a reason' preference is turned on

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

options.name: reason

options.description: (Optional) Filter for the SDK key's config

commands.name: scan
commands.description: Scan files for Feature Flag & Setting usages

options.name: config-id
options.description: ID of the Config to scan against

options.name: line-count
options.description: Context line count before and after the reference line (min: 1, max: 10)

options.name: repo
options.description: Repository name. Mandatory for code reference upload

options.name: branch
options.description: Branch name. When the scanned folder is a Git repo, it's determined automatically, otherwise, it must be set manually

options.name: commit-hash
options.description: Commit hash. When the scanned folder is a Git repo, it's determined automatically, otherwise, it must be set manually

options.name: file-url-template
options.description: Template url used to generate VCS file links. Available template parameters: `commitHash`, `filePath`, `lineNumber`. Example: https://github.com/my/repo/blob/{commitHash}/{filePath}#L{lineNumber}

options.name: commit-url-template
options.description: Template url used to generate VCS commit links. Available template parameters: `commitHash`. Example: https://github.com/my/repo/commit/{commitHash}

options.name: runner
options.description: Overrides the default `ConfigCat CLI {version}` executor label on the ConfigCat dashboard

options.name: exclude-flag-keys
options.description: Exclude the given Feature Flag keys from scanning. E.g.: `-ex flag1 flag2` or `-ex 'flag1,flag2'`

options.name: alias-patterns
options.description: List of custom regex patterns used to search for additional aliases

options.name: usage-patterns
options.description: List of custom regex patterns that describe additional feature flag key usages

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: eval
commands.description: Evaluate feature flags. In case of a single feature flag, by default, the command writes only the evaluated value to the output. In case of multiple feature flags, the command writes a table if no other format is specified

options.name: sdk-key
options.description: SDK key identifying the config to download, also loaded from CONFIGCAT_SDK_KEY

options.name: user-attributes
options.description: User attributes for flag evaluation. Format: `<key>:<value>`. Dedicated User Object attributes are mapped like the following: Identifier => id, Email => email, Country => country

options.name: base-url
options.description: The CDN base url from where the CLI will download the config JSON. Defaults to ConfigCat CDN servers

options.name: data-governance
options.description: Describes the location of your feature flag and setting data within the ConfigCat CDN

options.name: map
options.description: Format the output in semicolon delimited map: <key1>=<value1>;<key2>=<value2>

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: whoisthebestcat
commands.description: Well, who?

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: config-json
commands.description: Config JSON-related utilities

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: convert
commands.description: Convert between config JSON versions

options.name: hash-map
options.description: A JSON file containing the original values of hashed comparison valuesin the following format: '{"<hash>":"<original-value>"}'.

options.name: skip-salt
options.description: Skip writing salt into the converted JSON if it would be unused.

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

commands.name: get
commands.description: Download a config JSON from the CDN servers.

options.name: eu
options.description: Use the ConfigCat CDN servers located in the EU. Specify this option if you enabled EU Only data governance.

options.name: test
options.description: Use the ConfigCat CDN servers of the test infrastructure.

options.name: base-url
options.description: Use the server accessible at the specified URL. Specify this option if you set up a proxy server.

options.name: non-interactive
options.description: Turn off progress rendering and interactive features

arguments.name: sdk-key
arguments.description: The SDK key identifying the config to download.
